item.title: A Bitcoin Evangelist Tries to Convert Me
item.description: What the crypto currency promises that the dollar doesn’t.

item.title: There Is Much More to Pope vs. President Than Meets the Eye
item.description: What fighting an unjust war really means.

item.title: Tim Cook Did Wonders for Apple. He Also Did a Lot for Xi Jinping.
item.description: Tim Cook made Apple a juggernaut, but he did so by risking America’s economic and political security.

item.title: And Then There Were None, Trump Administration Edition
item.description: Normal language is inadequate and precedents are irrelevant when it comes to appraising the senior administration officials of the president’s second term.

item.title: How Bobby Pulido Could Bring Texas Latinos Back to the Democrats
item.description: Big parts of the Rio Grande Valley turned against the party in recent years. One candidate is trying to win it back, one quinceañera at a time.

item.title: A Bitcoin Evangelist Tries to Convert Me
item.description: What the crypto currency promises that the dollar doesn’t.

item.title: Israel Is Weaponizing Lebanon’s Diversity
item.description: Israel isn’t just targeting Hezbollah in the latest war. It is also targeting the multireligious fabric of Lebanese society.

item.title: I’ve Photographed Protests Around the World. This Still Surprises Me.
item.description: Scenes from life in a global era increasingly shaped by confrontations with authoritarian power.

item.title: Hollywood Movies Like ‘Project Hail Mary’ Are Too Long These Days
item.description: “Project Hail Mary” is just the latest overlong blockbuster.

item.title: ‘The Rich Don’t Play by the Rules. So Why Should I?’
item.description: Why petty theft might be the new political protest.

item.title: The Iran War’s Threats to Oil Are Boosting Green Energy
item.description: How the war’s disruptions are accelerating the shift to green energy.

item.title: Does Israel Want to Be Saudi Arabia?
item.description: Americans hold Israel to a higher standard than their other allies. That’s a good thing.

item.title: ‘The Rich Don’t Play by the Rules. So Why Should I?’
item.description: Why petty theft might be the new political protest.

item.title: The Wealthy Steal, Too — Just Differently
item.description: America’s social contract has broken down, Hasan Piker argues, and the wealthy steal, too — just in ways that the system protects. So can shoplifting by the 99 percent be a form of political protest? Watch this episode of “The Opinions” to hear the debate.

item.title: Would You Steal From Whole Foods?
item.description: Would you steal from Whole Foods? On “The Opinions,” Jia Tolentino and Hasan Piker join the Opinion culture editor Nadja Spiegelman for a candid conversation about microlooting, petty theft and the moral lines people draw, from shoplifting from big corporations to whether they’d ever dine and dash.

item.title: Yale Has Come Up With a Surefire Way to Make a Terrible Situation Worse
item.description: Don’t retreat from the world; engage it.

item.title: Americans Have Fled to Red States. Blue States Can Win Them Back.
item.description: Jason Furman, Neera Tanden and Elizabeth Wilkins discuss.

item.title: Four Old Women Share Their Wisdom
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Roger Rosenblatt. Also: The pianist András Schiff shows a way to protest.

item.title: There’s Another Reason Gen Z Can’t Find Work
item.description: It’s not just because of A.I. This started 40 years ago.

item.title: Iran Can Wait Out Trump’s Blockade
item.description: Trump is betting on a quick victory, but Iran’s higher pain threshold means his blockade will prolong the war.

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Coming After Birth Control Access in a Terrifying New Way
item.description: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.

item.title: The Economic Cost of Donald Trump’s Constant Chaos
item.description: Nonstop political chaos is badly damaging our economy.

item.title: Graham Platner Went to Hell and Back. He Has a Simple Message for Democrats.
item.description: At campaign stops, he talks about high gas prices and Trump’s incompetence. But the core of his message is an unflinching disgust for forever war.

item.title: The Sam Altman Attack Was Wrong. It Also Says Something Important About Silicon Valley.
item.description: The attack against Sam Altman reflects how badly Silicon Valley needs to address the societal consequences of its products.

item.title: Older Americans Are Hoarding America’s Potential
item.description: Older Americans need incentives to give up accumulated housing, jobs and wealth.

item.title: Trump Corrupts, and Absolute Trump Corrupts Absolutely
item.description: The president is one of a kind.

item.title: In 1968, They Saw Earth From the Moon for the First Time
item.description: It’s the most iconic image of our planet. Decades before the Artemis II mission, a group of astronauts experienced an Earth rising for the first time. This Earth Day, hear their story and the story of a photograph that changed the way we think about our place in the universe.

item.title: My Child Died of Measles
item.description: If more people had gotten the vaccine, she might still be here.

item.title: The Hypocrisy of OpenAI and Palantir
item.description: A super PAC that’s funded by co-founders of Palantir, OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz is spending millions on attack ads opposing Alex Bores’s campaign for Congress. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the New York state assemblyman explains how tech companies have targeted his pro-regulation positions, despite publicly claiming to welcome A.I. oversight.

item.title: A Real Plan for A.I. Job Losses
item.description: What if the A.I. boom takes not just your job, but most jobs? The congressional candidate Alex Bores joins the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein to pitch an “A.I. Dividend” — a policy devised to fund a universal basic income if A.I. begins to outmatch human labor.

item.title: David Zaslav Ate Hollywood
item.description: As the sale of Warner Bros. Discovery to Paramount Skydance hurtles toward board approval, David Zaslav is about to be more than an accidental mogul.

item.title: How to Save Academia
item.description: A Yale report offers some honest self-reflection on where the university went wrong.

item.title: How Israel Lost Its Way and How Trump Can Save Lebanon
item.description: The smashing of a statue of Jesus by an Israeli soldier and the founding of four provocative settlements together show Israel’s thoughtless path forward.

item.title: Democrats Look for a New Campaign Script
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by David Plouffe. Also: Why mothers are burned out; national referendums.

item.title: Live Nation. Amazon. Americans Are Standing Up to Corporate Lawbreaking.
item.description: Lina Khan and Doha Mekki on three court rulings that illustrate the power juries and local officials have to enforce antitrust laws.

item.title: Anthropic Wants Claude to Be Moral. Is Religion Really the Answer?
item.description: A.I. cannot meditate, fast or prostrate itself in prayer.

item.title: Putin’s Army of Drones
item.description: Freed from ethical constraints and hardened by years of war, a country long seen as a bureaucratic giant has become an entrepreneurial one.

item.title: Trump Is ‘Easily the Worst President in U.S. History'
item.description: There is a contest Trump is winning.

item.title: Can Kevin Warsh Pass the Jerome Powell Test?
item.description: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.

item.title: You Can’t Defend a Nation When Soldiers Don’t Have Child Care
item.description: Trump wants to pay for the military, not child care. History shows that the armed services are at their best only when soldiers’ kids are cared for.

item.title: Why Are Palantir and OpenAI Scared of Alex Bores?
item.description: Alex Bores is trying to win a congressional seat to represent part of New York City. Here’s why A.I. executives are funding the effort to stop him.

item.title: Why Are Palantir and OpenAI Scared of Alex Bores?
item.description: Alex Bores is trying to win a congressional seat to represent part of New York City. Here’s why A.I. executives are funding the effort to stop him.

item.title: Nearly Half the World Is Bearing the Brunt of Global Disorder. Don’t Expect Richer Countries to Get a Pass.
item.description: The Iran war is just the latest setback in the past several years to low-income and lower-middle-income countries.

item.title: Trump Is Losing His Cheerleaders
item.description: The columnist Michelle Goldberg normally doesn’t see much hope in the Trump era, but on “The Opinions,” she says the cracks in MAGA’s media machine are real — and explains what they could mean for Trump’s future.

item.title: The Founding Father Has Issues With the Office of the Presidency
item.description: He couldn’t get out of political office fast enough.

item.title: The Town That Reveals All of Trump’s Bad Economic Ideas
item.description: The jobs are coming back, despite President Trump’s tariffs and harsh immigration enforcement.

item.title: #MeToo and the Fight to End Sex Abuse in Congress
item.description: Readers discuss Eric Swalwell and sexual abuse in Congress. Also: Waning MAGA pride; ice cream and a lap cat as distractions.

item.title: The Divine Right of Presidents Is a Dangerous Idea
item.description: It didn’t work out too well for kings, either.

item.title: Times Are Hard. What’s a Teacher to Do?
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion essay by Frank Bruni. Also: An arch near Arlington; cuts in mental health care; transformation in prison.
